title: calendula incana subsp. maritima
intro: Calendula incana subsp. maritima, also known as sea pot marigold, is a subspecies of the calendula incana plant, a type of daisy-like flowering plant native to the coastal areas of Europe and Western Asia. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Calendula incana subsp. maritima include wound healing, skin regeneration, and anti-aging, making it a popular ingredient in skincare products and herbal remedies. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infused oils, salves, and creams to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall skin health and well-being.
